description:
secretx-systemd/src/lib.rs:96-103 and :108-118 distinguish three failure modes correctly:

  - SecretError::Unavailable when $CREDENTIALS_DIRECTORY is unset (infrastructure-not-configured; retry will not help, but this is a recoverable user-facing situation distinct from 'the credential is missing')
  - SecretError::NotFound when the credential file does not exist (the systemd unit is configured but this credential was not requested in LoadCredential=)
  - SecretError::Backend for any other I/O error (permission, EISDIR, EACCES, etc.)

This three-way split is correct in this backend (all three failure modes are distinguishable from the single tokio::fs::read syscall + an env-var read) and gives callers actionable information. Defend against any 'collapse Unavailable into Backend' simplification — Unavailable is documented to mean 'retrying may succeed', and the operator response is different (configure systemd, vs. fix the unit file, vs. file a bug).
title: GOOD: distinct Unavailable / NotFound / Backend error variants
